summary: Frontend verification of the _parse_iso offset-naive datetime fix in billing_readiness_router. Logged in as doctor@ccms.app, opened all 5 requested personas (Hannah Whitaker, Isabella Cho, Derrick Stone, Claire Morgan, Ethan Parker), navigated to the Clinical tab. Result: for EVERY persona the Clinical tab renders cleanly with ZERO 'Internal server error' toasts, ZERO HTTP 5xx responses captured at the browser layer, and all required sections present (Episodes, Initial Exam, Treatment Plan, Follow-up, Diagnoses, Re-Exam). Backend error log (/var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log) shows no new writes during the test window — last line is at 20:53 (Redis warning), tests ran 20:57-20:59. The iter 65 Clinical-tab 500 regression is CONFIRMED FIXED. Scheduling calendar shows past + future appointments for the personas (Jaxon Morgan, Derrick Stone, Ethan Parker, Marcus Reid, Hannah Whitaker, Claire Morgan all visible in the week of Apr 19-25 2026 view). Patients list: GET /api/patients returns all 8 expected personas (Ethan Parker, Hannah Whitaker, Marcus Reid, Isabella Cho, Derrick Stone, Aria Johnson, Claire Morgan, Jaxon Morgan).

minor.issue: ccms.cache repeatedly warns 'invalidate_prefix(patients:) failed: Error 111 connecting to 127.0.0.1:6379' — Redis is down. Non-blocking (cache invalidation only) but noisy.

action_items: None required for the reported bug — _parse_iso UTC-normalisation fix fully resolves the Clinical tab 500.
(Nice-to-have, not blocking) Either start Redis in the preview env or silence the repeated cache-invalidation warnings that now clutter backend.err.log.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix in billing_readiness_router._parse_iso (naive -> UTC) is tight and localised; good defensive coding. Consider applying the same normalisation helper wherever stored YYYY-MM-DD dates are compared against tz-aware encounter.date_of_service values, to avoid the bug re-appearing in a sibling router.

Clinical tab still silently degrades on sub-fetch failures (observed in iter 65 pre-fix). Consider per-section error boundaries + surfacing the correlation id in a dev-friendly way so regressions are attributed to the right section rather than a generic top-level toast.

Search result rows use data-testid='search-result-{uuid}' which is stable. Consider also adding a deterministic data-testid like 'patient-result-{last-name-slug}' to make test automation selectors more readable and resilient across re-seeds.

success_rate.backend: 100% (no 5xx captured, err log unchanged during test window)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 personas render Clinical tab with all sections, no error toast)

test_credentials: doctor@ccms.app / Doctor@ComplianceClinic1 (no MFA). Break-glass reason modal handled with 'Clinical review retest iter66 — verify fix' then Record & Continue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Riverbend clinical-workflow Clinical tab 500 regression is fully resolved after _parse_iso fix. All 8 personas present via /api/patients. Scheduling renders past+future appts. If further bugs surface on Clinical tab, first tail /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log with the correlation id from the toast — that is the fastest path from UI error to server stack. Patient search uses [data-testid="search-q"] and first result is [data-testid^="search-result-"]; clicking it triggers the break-glass 'Open patient record' modal which requires 8+ char reason then 'Record & Continue'.

rca of the issue.summary: Iter 65 Clinical-tab 500 (for active-episode personas) caused by billing_readiness_router._parse_iso returning a naive datetime for YYYY-MM-DD plan.re_exam_date values, which then failed comparison against tz-aware encounter.date_of_service.
rca of the issue.fix_verification: Normalising naive datetimes to UTC inside _parse_iso (per agent-context note — line 164) eliminates the 500 for Hannah/Isabella/Derrick. Closed-episode (Claire) and maintenance (Ethan) continue to load cleanly as before, so no regression introduced.
rca of the issue.reproduction_before_fix: login doctor@ccms.app -> /patients -> search 'Whitaker' -> click result -> break-glass reason -> Record & Continue -> Clinical tab -> red 'Internal server error. Contact support with the correlation id below.' toast appeared top-right within ~2s.
rca of the issue.reproduction_after_fix: Same steps — NO toast appears, all chart sections render (Episodes, Initial Exam, Treatment Plan, Follow-up, Diagnoses, Re-Exam), no 5xx network responses, and backend.err.log shows no new entries during the test window.
